rulings.Case Title: RICK W GRIMSLEY VS. MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, Arpil 9, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY, and, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT,AND, OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. The 2002-2003 policies are the only policies at issue. The trigger of coverage with the single event of the accident in September 2002 resulting in immediate injury. The policy changes occurred in July 2002, prior to the accident. No triable issue of fact is raised. =(302CWW/PB)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, Arpil 9, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Motion granted. =(302CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: VIRGINIA BALDOVIESO VS. KEN WOO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, Arpil 9, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Demurrer to causes of action for Truth in Lending Act violations and Declaratory Relief not opposed. Claims related to the 2004 loan transaction time-barred. Facts not alleged regarding CitiMortgage's negligence, or its fraudulent representations, or that Woo is Citimortgage's agent. =(302CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: FELIX BRAYNIN et al VS. VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, Arpil 9, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT BIANA CHERNOGUZ, and, VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action. Demurrer overruled as to the first and second causes of action. Ten days leave to answer. =(302CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JANIE COLLINS VS. SUPERIOR COURT OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, Arpil 9, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, no opposition filed. Ten days leave to amend. =(302CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JAY ELLIOT VS. DATA TRANSFER OF NEVADA, LLC, FORMERLY MIGO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, Arpil 9, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT DATA TRANSFER OF NEVADA, LLC, FORMERLY MIGO, KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, and, ROBERT HALLIGAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, second, and third causes of action. Plaintiff must plead with greater specificity facts that show these claims are not barred by the separation agreement. Demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action. =(302CWW/JW)
